denial_pattern_sources: superseded_guideline: AHIP 2022 Coverage Policy Review; documented in multiple Cigna litigation cases 2021–2023
plan_exclusion_overrides_state_mandate: NCSL State Health Insurance Mandates Database 2024; documented in CA, NJ, IL enforcement actions
denial_letter_text:
Dear Plan Member,

We received your provider's request on 06/20/2026 for Multidisciplinary concussion clinic program related to Post-concussion syndrome (G44.309). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) criteria and InterQual review standards. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. A lower level of care is appropriate for Post-concussion syndrome (G44.309); Multidisciplinary concussion clinic program exceeds what is required. Outpatient management has not been exhausted per clinical guidelines.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) and utilization-management requirements associated with level of care too high. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
clinical_context: The 19-year-old member has Post-concussion syndrome (G44.309). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Multidisciplinary concussion clinic program recommended as the next appropriate step. Headache and dizziness 3 months post-MVA; vestibular therapy started. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that level of care too high bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.
